channel.title: Patient Support & Services on Tokyo Police Hospital
channel.description: Recent content in Patient Support & Services on Tokyo Police Hospital

item.title: General Information & Reception
item.description:
General Information Desk

The General Information Desk is located in the main lobby on the 1st Floor, near the main entrance.

Our friendly staff are available to assist with:

  • Directing you to the correct department or clinic
  • Providing information about hospital services
  • Lending wheelchairs and strollers
  • Answering general questions about the hospital

Wheelchair & Stroller Loan

The following mobility aids are available free of charge from the Information Desk:

Item Quantity Available
Wheelchairs 12
Strollers (for young children) 2

Please return items to the Information Desk after use.

item.title: Medical Documents & Certificates
item.description:
Medical Documents & Certificates

We provide a range of official medical documents for patients, including medical certificates, diagnosis reports, summaries, and insurance-related forms.

Document Counter
Location Counter ⑦, 1st Floor
Phone 03-5343-5670
Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:00 – 17:00

Types of Documents Available

Document Type Notes
Medical Certificate (Shindan-sho) General health/diagnosis certificate
Diagnosis Summary Summary of your condition and treatment
Discharge Summary Issued at or after discharge
Certificate of Surgery Record of surgical procedure
Insurance Claim Form For private insurance companies
Disability Certificate Requires physician review
Return-to-Work Certificate Confirmation of fitness to return to work
School/Work Absence Certificate Confirmation of illness for absence

Application Procedure

  1. Visit Counter ⑦ on the 1st Floor during business hours, or request by mail.
  2. Complete the Application Form available at the counter (or downloadable from the hospital’s Japanese website).
  3. Present your:
    • Patient registration card
    • Photo ID (if collecting on behalf of patient, bring authorization letter and your own ID)
  4. Pay the applicable fee.
  5. Wait approximately 2 weeks for most documents to be prepared.
  6. You will be notified when your document is ready for collection.

Processing Time

Standard processing time is approximately 2 weeks from the date of application.

item.title: Second Opinion Service
item.description:
Second Opinion Service

If you have received a diagnosis or treatment recommendation from another hospital and wish to have it reviewed by one of our specialists, Tokyo Police Hospital offers a Second Opinion Consultation Service.

A second opinion can help you:

  • Confirm or clarify your diagnosis
  • Understand your treatment options more fully
  • Gain confidence in your care plan
  • Explore alternative approaches

Key Requirements

  • A referral letter from your current physician is required. This confirms your diagnosis, current treatment plan, and the specific question(s) you want addressed.
  • All consultations are by appointment only.
  • Second opinion consultations are not covered by public health insurance and must be paid in full by the patient.

Fees

Consultation Duration Fee
Up to 30 minutes ¥15,000 (tax included)
30 minutes – 60 minutes ¥30,000 (tax included)

Fees are paid in advance at the time of booking confirmation. Additional charges may apply if special tests or diagnostic review are required.

item.title: Nutrition Counseling
item.description:
Nutrition Counseling & Guidance

Our team of registered dietitians provides personalized nutrition counseling and education for patients with a variety of health conditions. Good nutrition is a fundamental component of disease prevention, treatment, and recovery.

Individual Nutrition Counseling

Individual consultations are available for patients whose conditions require specific dietary management, including:

  • Diabetes mellitus – carbohydrate management, meal planning, blood glucose control
  • Kidney disease (CKD) – protein, potassium, phosphorus, and fluid management
  • Hypertension – sodium restriction, DASH diet
  • Dyslipidemia (high cholesterol) – fat intake, heart-healthy diet
  • Obesity – calorie management, behavioral change support
  • Cancer and cancer treatment – nutritional support during chemotherapy/radiation
  • Post-surgical nutrition – support for recovery and dietary progression
  • Eating disorders – supportive nutritional counseling
  • Allergies and special diets – food allergy management

How to Arrange

Individual counseling is arranged through your attending physician or the outpatient clinic. Please ask your doctor for a dietitian referral.

item.title: Medical Social Work
item.description:
Medical Welfare Consultation Office (Medical Social Work)

Our Medical Social Workers (MSWs) are available to provide free, confidential support to patients and their families on a wide range of non-medical concerns that arise during illness and hospitalization.

Hours & Contact
Hours Monday – Saturday, 8:30 – 17:00
Location 1st Floor (ask at the Information Desk or nursing station)
Phone 03-5343-5611 (main) → ask for Medical Welfare Consultation Office

All consultations are free of charge and strictly confidential.

item.title: Medical Records Disclosure
item.description:
Medical Records Disclosure

In accordance with Japanese law and our hospital’s patient rights policy, patients have the right to access and obtain copies of their own medical records. We handle all requests in compliance with the Act on the Protection of Personal Information.

Application Procedure

  1. Submit a completed Medical Records Disclosure Application Form (available at the Document Counter ⑦, 1F, or on the hospital’s Japanese website).
  2. Present valid photo ID (passport, driver’s license, or My Number card with photo).
  3. If applying on behalf of a patient:
    • Written authorization from the patient
    • Applicant’s own photo ID
    • Relationship documentation if applicable (family register, etc.)
  4. Pay the applicable fees.
  5. Records will be prepared and made available within approximately 2–4 weeks of application.

Fees

Item Fee
Application handling fee ¥3,300 per request (tax included)
Paper medical record copy ¥33 per page (tax included)
Electronic medical record copy ¥22 per page (tax included)
Diagnostic image DVD (per disc) ¥1,100 per disc (tax included)

All fees must be paid in advance. Fees are non-refundable once processing has begun.

item.title: Multidisciplinary Support Teams
item.description:
Multidisciplinary Support Teams

Tokyo Police Hospital operates a comprehensive network of multidisciplinary support teams that work across all departments to ensure the highest standards of patient safety, care quality, and clinical outcomes.

Each team brings together specialists from different disciplines — physicians, nurses, pharmacists, dietitians, therapists, and others — to provide expert support to clinical teams and patients throughout the hospital.

ICT — Infection Control Team

The Infection Control Team (ICT) monitors, prevents, and manages hospital-acquired infections throughout the facility.

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
item.description:
Frequently Asked Questions

Outpatient & First Visit

Q: Do I need a referral letter to visit Tokyo Police Hospital? A: You do not need a referral letter to visit our outpatient clinic. However, patients visiting without a referral letter will be charged an additional initial consultation surcharge of ¥7,700 (tax included). We strongly encourage obtaining a referral letter from your regular physician to avoid this charge and to facilitate more efficient care.

item.title: Patient Safety
item.description:
Patient Safety at Tokyo Police Hospital

Patient safety is the highest priority at Tokyo Police Hospital. We maintain a comprehensive, hospital-wide safety management program to prevent errors, identify risks, and continuously improve the quality of care we deliver.

Our Safety Management System

Safety Management Committee

The Patient Safety Management Committee oversees all aspects of safety across the hospital. This committee includes the hospital director, department heads, nursing leadership, pharmacists, and other key staff.

item.title: Mobile Phone Policy
item.description:
Mobile Phone & Electronic Device Policy

To protect the privacy of all patients and to prevent interference with medical equipment, Tokyo Police Hospital has established the following rules for the use of mobile phones, smartphones, tablets, and laptops.

Permitted Areas

Mobile phone use is permitted in the following areas:

  • Lobby and corridor areas on the 1st Floor
  • Designated patient lounge areas on ward floors
  • Convenience store and café area (Natural Lawson, Tully’s Coffee)
  • Outdoor areas on hospital grounds (excluding smoking restrictions)

Prohibited Areas

Mobile phone use is strictly prohibited in the following areas:

item.title: Suggestions & Feedback
item.description:
Suggestions & Feedback

Tokyo Police Hospital values every comment, suggestion, and concern from our patients, families, and visitors. Your feedback is essential for helping us improve our services and the quality of care we provide.

Suggestion Boxes

Suggestion boxes are located at the following locations throughout the hospital:

  • Main lobby (1st Floor, near Information Desk)
  • Outpatient waiting areas (2F, 3F)
  • Ward entrances (5F–9F)

Submission forms and pens are available next to each box. You may submit anonymously, or include your contact information if you would like a response.

item.title: Assistance Dogs Policy
item.description:
Assistance Dogs at Tokyo Police Hospital

In accordance with the Act for Assistance Dog Users (Houshin Jiritsushien Hou), Tokyo Police Hospital welcomes and accommodates users of all certified assistance dogs, including:

  • Guide Dogs (Mōdōken) — for persons with visual impairments
  • Service Dogs / Mobility Assistance Dogs (Kaitejoken) — for persons with physical disabilities
  • Hearing Dogs (Hochōken) — for persons who are deaf or hard of hearing

Certified assistance dogs are permitted in all areas of the hospital that their user needs to access, including outpatient areas, wards, and support areas.

item.title: Clinical Trials & Research
item.description:
Clinical Trials & Medical Research

Tokyo Police Hospital is committed to advancing medical knowledge and improving patient care through rigorous clinical research. We participate in clinical trials and observational studies in collaboration with academic institutions, research organizations, and pharmaceutical companies.

What Are Clinical Trials?

Clinical trials are research studies that test new treatments, medications, diagnostic procedures, or care approaches in human participants. They are conducted in accordance with strict ethical guidelines and regulatory requirements to ensure patient safety and scientific validity.
